evals.prompt: Audit the SEO for https://example.com as if it were our SaaS homepage. I care most about technical SEO, snippets, and what to fix before we run paid traffic to it.
evals.expected_output: A prioritized SEO audit that states live-site limitations, separates confirmed observations from assumptions, checks indexability and metadata first, and gives actionable fixes instead of a generic checklist.
expectations: The report includes an explicit scope or limitations statement.
Findings are prioritized by severity or expected SEO impact.
Each major issue includes evidence, why it matters, and a recommended fix.

evals.prompt: Review the SEO implementation in my Next.js app before launch. The homepage is app/page.tsx, metadata is in app/layout.tsx, and we have app/robots.ts plus app/sitemap.ts. Focus on framework-specific problems and code-level fixes.
evals.expected_output: A source-code-oriented SEO review that looks for Next.js metadata, robots, sitemap, canonical, structured data, rendering, image, and route issues without claiming deployed behavior.
expectations: The output avoids claiming live deployment behavior unless a live URL is provided.
The output checks Next.js-specific SEO mechanisms such as metadata, robots.ts, sitemap.ts, and rendering mode.
Recommended fixes include implementation notes or code-oriented guidance.

evals.prompt: Can you review this product page HTML for ecommerce SEO and AI search readiness? I want to know if the title, headings, schema, product details, trust signals, and page structure are good enough to rank.
evals.expected_output: A product-page SEO review that covers metadata, headings, Product/Breadcrumb schema, content depth, trust signals, internal links, accessibility, and AI-search readability.
expectations: The output evaluates ecommerce-specific schema and trust signals.
The output includes AI search readiness criteria without treating them as a separate generic checklist.
The final roadmap orders fixes from highest impact to lowest impact.
